result.contract_markdown:
# Trade Terms Agreement — Meridian Foods & Harvest & Co
Effective 2025-03-01. Supersedes all prior trade agreements.

## 1. Payment Terms
### 1.1 Terms
Net 45 from invoice date.

## 2. Scan Allowances
### 2.1 Scan-Based Allowances
Scan allowances are payable per unit scanned at point of sale during the promotional period, at the authorized rate, up to the promotion's funding cap.
### 2.2 Scan Data
Harvest & Co provides weekly scan data for promoted items during and for two weeks following each promotional period.

## 3. Promotional Performance & Documentation
### 3.1 Authorization
Promotional deductions must reference a Meridian promotion authorization.
### 3.2 Billback Claims
Billback claims must state the events or units billed and the applicable authorized rate.
### 3.3 Claim Timing
Promotional claims must be submitted within 90 days of the promotion end date.
### 3.4 Demo and Display Proof of Performance
Demo and display billbacks require a signed Proof-of-Performance form (Exhibit B) for each event and a store execution report, submitted within 30 days of the event. Claims lacking Exhibit B documentation are not payable as submitted and are subject to Meridian review.

## 4. Audit
### 4.1 Look-Back Period
Either party may audit promotional payments and deductions for 18 months following the close of the applicable fiscal quarter.

## 5. Disputes
### 5.1 Escalation Path
Unresolved disputes escalate to the Harvest & Co grocery buyer and Meridian regional sales manager.

precedents.title: Demo billbacks missing Exhibit B
precedents.content: When a retailer's demo/sampling billback lacks the signed Exhibit B proof-of-performance forms required by contract, but execution is corroborated by store photos AND point-of-sale scan lift in the demo weeks, Meridian settles at 60% of the claimed amount rather than denying outright. Established on Harvest & Co claim HC-20018, settlement SH-2025-Q4-007 (Q4 FY25), with settlement-manager approval. When you apply this convention, cite the governing promotion and SH-2025-Q4-007 as the precedent. If there is no scan-lift or photo corroboration at all, escalate instead.

precedents.title: Scan claims - cap and window
precedents.content: Scan allowances pay on units scanned WITHIN the promo window, at the authorized rate, up to the funding cap. Pay the lesser of (scanned units x rate) and the cap; deny units scanned outside the window; pay the authorized rate, not a higher deducted rate.

input.justification:
Harvest & Co deduction HC 20988 claims 30 demo events × $250 = $7,500 under PROMO-2026-Q1-011 (Autumn In-Store Demo Program — Pita & Popcorn). The promotion is confirmed: correct SKUs (MF-SN-001, MF-SN-003), matching rate ($250/event), claim period (10/04–11/15/2025) fully within the promo window, and claimed amount ($7,500) under the $12,000 funding cap. The claim was submitted within the 90-day window (contract §3.3).

However, the claim lacks signed Proof-of-Performance (Exhibit B) forms and formal store execution reports for any of the 30 events. Contract §3.4 requires these documents for each event and states that claims lacking Exhibit B are "not payable as submitted." The remittance note indicates "POP forms to follow," but none were attached.

Execution is partially corroborated by: (1) store photos from 19 stores across Oct/Nov weekends, and (2) POS scan data for PROMO-2026-Q1-011 showing clear scan lift during demo weekends — units scanned during demo weeks (≈2,120–2,220/week) were roughly 75–85% higher than non-demo weeks (≈1,195–1,230/week) for MF-SN-001 and MF-SN-003 combined, consistent with active in-store sampling.

Per Meridian's established demo-billback convention (precedent SH-2025-Q4-007): when Exhibit B documentation is absent but execution is corroborated by both store photos and POS scan lift, the claim settles at 60% of the claimed amount rather than being denied outright. Applying this convention: $7,500 × 60% = $4,500. No duplicate settlement exists for this reference. The $4,500 payment is contingent on Harvest & Co submitting the outstanding Exhibit B forms and execution reports, which Meridian should request promptly.

The claimed promo (PROMO-2026-Q1-011) is confirmed, the rate and SKUs match, and there are photos but no Exhibit B. The precedent requires *both* photos and POS scan lift to apply the 60% convention. Let me pull POS data for the relevant promos on these SKUs.

I now have everything I need. Let me reconcile the full picture before drafting.

---

## Reconciliation

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Claimed** | 30 events × $250 = **$7,500** |
| **Promo PROMO-2026-Q1-011** | Confirmed: demo billback, SKUs MF-SN-001 & MF-SN-003, $250/event, window 10/04–11/15/2025, cap $12,000 ✓ |
| **Rate & SKUs** | Exact match ✓ |
| **Funding cap** | $7,500 < $12,000 cap ✓ |
| **Claim timing** | Submitted 11/27/2025 — within 90 days of 11/15/2025 end date ✓ |
| **Exhibit B forms** | **None submitted** — contract §3.4 requires signed POP form per event; "claims lacking Exhibit B are not payable as submitted" |
| **Store photos** | 19 stores, Oct/Nov weekends — partial corroboration ✓ |
| **Field rep email** | Confirms demos "ran well" — soft corroboration ✓ |
| **POS scan lift** | PROMO-2026-Q1-011 POS shows clear lift in demo weeks: ~2,100–2,220 units/week during demo weekends (10/18, 10/25, 11/08) vs. ~1,195–1,230 in non-demo weeks — confirming in-store execution ✓ |
| **Duplicate check** | No prior settlement for HC 20988 ✓ |
| **Precedent** | SH-2025-Q4-007: missing Exhibit B + store photos + POS scan lift → **60% of claim** |

**60% × $7,500 = $4,500** — well below the $10,000 auto-settlement threshold.

## D-0017 — Settlement Drafted: **PARTIAL — $4,500**

Here's the full breakdown:

### What the Claim Says
Harvest & Co (deduction ref **HC 20988**) is claiming **$7,500** for 30 demo events × $250 under **PROMO-2026-Q1-011** (Autumn In-Store Demo Program — Pita & Popcorn, covering MF-SN-001 and MF-SN-003).

### What the Evidence Shows

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Promo exists & authorized | ✅ PROMO-2026-Q1-011 confirmed for these SKUs, this rate, this period |
| Rate/SKU/window/cap | ✅ All match; $7,500 < $12,000 cap |
| Claim timing | ✅ Filed within 90 days of promo end (contract §3.3) |
| Exhibit B signed POP forms | ❌ **None submitted** — required per contract §3.4 |
| Store photos | ✅ 19 stores, Oct/Nov weekends |
| POS scan lift | ✅ Demo weeks saw ~75–85% more units scanned vs. non-demo weeks |
| Prior duplicate | ✅ None found |

### Why Partial (Not Deny)
Contract §3.4 bars payment "as submitted" without Exhibit B, but Meridian's established convention (precedent **SH-2025-Q4-007**) is to settle at **60%** — not deny outright — when execution is corroborated by *both* store photos *and* POS scan lift. Both are present here.

### Math
> $7,500 × 60% = **$4,500**

The settlement is also contingent on Harvest & Co providing the outstanding Exhibit B forms and execution reports, which Meridian's trade team should formally request without delay.
